serving.natural_language_portion: One plate of Signature Crab Meat Scrambled Egg Fried Rice, typically a generous single adult serving of approximately 350g.

nutrient_source.title: Rice, fried, with egg, shrimp, and pork
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 172403, a similar fried rice dish with mixed proteins (egg, shrimp, pork) used as a proxy for crab meat and egg. Fields not reported in this source entry are set to -1 and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Fried Rice (white rice base)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index of 68 is estimated based on typical values for white rice fried rice dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database. No exact entry for Crab Meat Scrambled Egg Fried Rice was available. GI 68 falls within the medium category (56–69).

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its high sodium content (396 mg/100g), which can contribute significantly to elevated blood pressure when consumed in a full restaurant-sized portion.

type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be mindful of the carbohydrate content (20.3 g/100g) and medium glycemic index (68) of this dish, opting for smaller portions or pairing with non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Those with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation due to its saturated fat (1.54 g/100g) and cholesterol (66 mg/100g) content, which may adversely affect lipid profiles when consumed frequently or in large portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Signature Crab Meat Scrambled Egg Fried Rice (招牌蟹肉滑蛋炒饭) is a popular Chinese-Singaporean dish featuring fragrant wok-fried white rice combined with succulent crab meat and fluffy scrambled eggs, typically seasoned with soy sauce and garnished with spring onions. The rice is stir-fried at high heat to achieve 'wok hei' (breath of the wok), imparting a characteristic smoky aroma. Scrambled eggs are folded in towards the end of cooking to maintain a soft, silky texture that contrasts with the firm rice grains. The dish delivers a savory flavor profile with the natural sweetness of crab, richness of egg, and umami depth from soy sauce. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 173 kcal per 100g, with 6.94g protein, 7.08g fat, and 20.3g carbohydrate. Its medium glycemic index (estimated 68) and high sodium content (396 mg/100g) make portion control important for individuals managing blood pressure or blood sugar. It is a common lunch or dinner option in Singaporean seafood restaurants and is typically served as a shared dish for two to three persons.
common_user_queries: 招牌蟹肉滑蛋炒饭 calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from a similar USDA SR Legacy entry for fried rice with mixed proteins (FDC ID 172403). Glycemic index is an analogous estimate based on white rice fried rice from the University of Sydney GI Database. Multilingual names and ontology codes are well-researched. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match for this specific dish exists in an approved primary source.
